what: Authoritative gate (Definition of Done) — read before claiming green
title: CLAUDE.md
text: authoritative full-suite gate suite FAIL 0 / ERROR 0 / SKIP 0 / PASS N (N grows; last ≈3388 2026-05-29 — count gate, 0/0/0 ). Default run serial (~590s); devtools::test() sets NOT_CRAN=true browser tests RUN (0 SKIP): Parallelism opt-— use post-merge full-suite gate explicitly ask (never inner loop, never R CMD check). cuts 590s→270s; enable TESTTHAT_PARALLEL=TRUE TESTTHAT_CPUS=4 (cap 4 — browser tests render-bound, >4 concurrent Chromes oversubscribe CPU → slower boot-tail flakes). residual flake parallel load product failure — re-confirm serially calling regression. ⚠ Proxy trap — fooled (corrected 2026-05-17 / 2026-05-29, empirically verified): - raw Rscript -e 'testthat::test_dir()/test_file()' NOT_CRAN → skip_on_cran() fires → every shinytest2 browser test silently SKIPs. “green, SKIP n” gate. - Parallel runs (TESTTHAT_PARALLEL=TRUE): testthat::test_dir() defaults load_package="none", worker subprocesses don’t load ggpaintr (parent’s devtools::load_all() doesn’t propagate) → use devtools::test(), pkgloads source pkg per worker. Worker count = TESTTHAT_CPUS else getOption("Ncpus") else 2. Parallel exposes boot-tail races (renderUI input read/uploaded bindAll() wires ); e2e boot helpers hardened 4b5f4d0 (poll/await bindings + upload_file() wrapper). - devtools::check(... ---cran) make skip_on_cran() fire (devtools runs check subprocess NOT_CRAN=true). browser fixtures instead cleanly SKIP via boot_vignette_app()’s source-root guard (DESCRIPTION .Rcheck sandbox). check run shows e2e file ERRORing (R CMD check found ERRORs, pkgload “DESCRIPTION found”), guard missing/broken — harness defect, product failure, never gate. - NOT_CRAN=true / devtools::test() runs browser tests (source tree present → 0 SKIP). alone gate. Never claim suite green, merge branch, mark step done, clear context skipping/erroring harness. gates: devtools::check(document = FALSE, manual = FALSE, args = c("---cran","---manual")) → 0 errors / 0 warnings (-design NOTEs expected: CRAN incoming “new submission”, .git, top-level CONTEXT.md); e2e browser file cleanly SKIPs via source-root guard — non-zero-error check means guard regressed, product failure (see proxy-trap block ). Docs: source("dev/build-pkgdown.R"); build_pkgdown_clean() → clean. Browser e2e needs shinytest2 + chromote + Chrome; absent → clean -skip acceptable must reported, hidden. Every plan project takes Definition Done block. Audit/enforce plan /implementable <plan-path>.

title: ggpaintr — Domain Context
text: L1 — --one: standalone entry points build run whole app: ptr_app() (single formula), ptr_app_grid() (multiple formulas). user writes formula, gets running Shiny app. Avoid: “simple API”, “quick mode”. L2 — Embed default layout: “Plug ggpaintr Shiny app, accept default layout (fluidPage → sidebar controls + main plot/code/error), optionally restyle via CSS.” L2 entry points self-contained pair ptr_ui() / ptr_server() (formerly ptr_module_ui / ptr_module_server — renamed ADR 0006; ptr_server() single public server entry, used L2 L3) shared coordinator API ptr_shared() (coordinator) / ptr_shared_panel() (panel UI) / ptr_shared_server() (multi-plot cross-module sharing). non-ptr_ui/ptr_server split (ptr_controls_ui/ptr_outputs_ui) removed; shared pair orthogonal stays L2 public API. Avoid: “split”, “embedding API” (ambiguous L2 L3); “module pair” / “ptr_module_*” (renamed — say ptr_ui/ptr_server); reading trimmed split excluding shared pair. L3 — layout (UI-side split) — LOCKED 2026-05-16, amended 2026-05-18 (ADR 0006): L3 UI-side split : anything beyond default layout — break ptr_ui’s layout, place built-panes individually, replace pane widget, render path (plotly::renderPlotly() + plotlyOutput() instead ptr_ui_plot()). Composed bare pieces; L3 user supplies Shiny (optionally ptr_ui_page). L3 server-side concept. server always single public ptr_server(formula, id = NULL, …) — identical L2 L3; everything registered server-side; piece corresponding UI -op; built-plot/code/error read state$runtime(). Custom render = extract components state returned ptr_server() (ADR 0006; supersedes vignette’s “needs nothing beyond L2”, project-headless-removed memory’s “demoted L2 subsection” clause, ADR 0005’s “custom render owning render path” framing — -headless / -testServer--feature prohibition still stands). still L3 (place output widget) separate level server pattern: one server entry; read state$runtime()$plot return value. Canonical custom-render pattern: state <- ptr_server(formula, id) (inside host server, user’s widget shiny::NS(id)("my_plot") UI), output$my_plot <- plotly::renderPlotly(state$runtime()$plot). user-authored moduleServer wrapping ggpaintr engine public bare engine — namespacing handled ptr_server() . state server-side — never reachable UI code. state$server_ns_fn/ui_ns_fn internal plumbing, public — never documented user escape hatch. (Reason ADR 0006: single server entry custom render extracts returned state; old ptr_server-inside---moduleServer justification withdrawn internal-conclusion stands.) ptr_ui rebuilt L3 API: internal composition bare pieces + self-contained shell + combinators + (single-formula) auto inline shared section. ptr_server_internal (former exported ptr_server 4-arg engine) unexported plumbing — L1/L2/L3 surface, never recommended users. Avoid: “headless” (non-Shiny path — see CLAUDE.md / project memory), “custom rendering level” / “L3 server pattern” (custom render UI-side L3 fed one server entry, level server pattern), “render path” server concept, calling ptr_server “module” server implying sibling non-module server, telling users wire custom outputs state$server_ns_fn wrap bare engine moduleServer.

what: Shared placeholders — two distinct UI surfaces
title: ggpaintr — Domain Context
text: Shared placeholder: placeholder annotated … (shared = "<key>"). Every occurrence key — across aes, pipeline stages, (multi-plot) across formulas — driven one widget lockstep. Valid single-formula case (one key used multiple spots one plot) multi-formula case (one key across plots). Avoid: “linked input”, “global parameter”. shared section vs shared panel choice decided per key many formulas reference (partition rule — implemented: shared_partition() R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R computes panel_keys (≥2 formulas) vs formula-local, ownership applied end--end via host_owned_keys / panel_sources): shared key referenced exactly one formula → formula’s shared section. shared key referenced ≥2 formulas → one shared panel. Example: f1 = sharedA + sharedA + sharedB, f2 = sharedC + sharedC + sharedB ⟹ f1’s section holds sharedA, f2’s section holds sharedC, standalone panel holds sharedB. partition role-agnostic (ADR 0023): applies value placeholders, ppVar(shared=) consumers, sources (ppUpload(shared=)) alike. panel-owned source resolved host-side delivered every instance via panel_sources. One asymmetry illegal construction: panel-owned consumer requires panel-owned source — pairing shared consumer (≥2 formulas) formula-local sources parse-time error ptr_shared(), one shared column picker list columns two different datasets. Shared section (inline, formula-local): block shared-key widgets rendered inside one formula’s control panel, keys occur formula. Namespaced via controls’ ns (<id>-shared_<key>); driven formula’s ptr_server() (single public server entry) — top-level ptr_shared_server(). Automatic (formula formula-local shared keys gets one; user-triggered). internal render_shared_section flag toggles whether whole section renders; per-key partition applied — panel-owned keys (≥2 formulas) excluded inline section via host_owned_keys (ptr_bind_local_shared_consumers), key never renders section panel. Avoid: calling “panel”; assuming knows formulas. Shared panel (standalone, cross-formula): page-level shiny::wellPanel() built ptr_shared_panel(obj) (coordinator obj, raw formulas), holding keys referenced ≥2 formulas. ptr_shared(..., id = ...) namespaces every shared id coordinator’s id (<id>_shared_<key>, <id>_ptr_shared_draw_all, <id>_ptr_shared_errors) — see R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:220–234, 292. Multiple coordinators one page supported, id; server counterparts ptr_shared_server(obj) run top level, never inside moduleServer(id). Panel-owned shared sources (ppUpload(shared=) referenced ≥2 formulas) resolved host-side delivered instance via bundle’s panel_sources field (ptr_setup_panel_sources(), ADR 0023) — source-role gap ADR 0005 left open now closed. Avoid: calling “section”; assuming single coordinator id mandatory; assuming panel currently filters cross-formula keys (today takes keys).

(expr_check needed translates formulas find shared keys; id namespaces panel multiple coordinators can coexist one page. former shared_ui per-key widget-override argument removed — every shared key now auto-renders via placeholder’s build_ui.) computes partition (per key: formula-local → formula’s section; ≥2 formulas → panel) single source truth UI server can never disagree. Consumed exactly three functions, take obj (duplicated config): - ptr_shared_panel(obj, css = NULL) — L2 self-contained panel (css = L2 restyle hook). - ptr_ui_shared_panel(obj) — L3 bare panel (css; L3 shell owns assets). - ptr_shared_server(obj) — reactive ptr_shared_state bundle, unchanged downstream ptr_server(shared_state=). Avoid: deriving partition independently anywhere else; treating obj reactive; putting ui_text config panel functions; referring shared_ui argument (removed). Shared usage instance count — LOCKED 2026-05-16: - One ggpaintr instance ⟹ shared=obj; shared widgets auto-render instance’s inline shared section. coordinator, panel — ever. (Single-instance “promote standalone panel prominence” considered rejected.) - Multiple instances ⟹ user must build obj <- ptr_shared(formulas=list(…)); cross-formula keys (≥2) go one ptr_shared_panel(obj); formula-local keys still render instance’s inline section. - Therefore ptr_shared / ptr_shared_panel / ptr_shared_server strictly multi-instance API; single-instance embedders never see . old ptr_ui(shared_panel=) boolean removed — routing governed solely presence shared=obj + count-based partition. Shared coordinator consumers — exactly two roles, never : - ptr_ui_controls(id, formula, shared = obj) — renders layer controls plus formula’s formula-local shared section, excluding obj’s panel-owned keys. (Decision 2026-05-16: section folded controls piece, separate placeable piece.) - shared-panel piece — renders exactly obj’s ≥2-formula keys. - pieces (ptr_ui_plot/ptr_ui_error/ptr_ui_code/header) shared-agnostic — never take obj. - Single-formula L3, panel → omit obj; controls piece renders formula’s shared keys (every key formula-local definition).

what: Layer and stage toggles — pp* structural-keyword family
title: ggpaintr — Domain Context
text: pp* family two roles, registered registry routed differently translator: Placeholders (role = "value" / "consumer" / "source"): ppText, ppNum, ppExpr, ppVar, ppUpload. Become ptr_ph_* nodes; UI widget bind-point. Naked-R semantics: identity (return arg unchanged) formula renders --ggpaintr . Structural keywords (role = "structural"): ppLayerOff, ppVerbSwitch. Never become ptr_ph_* nodes — translator’s special-unwrap branches recognise wrapper, translate inner expression normal layer/stage, stamp metadata resulting carrier node. wrapper never appears typed tree. Avoid: calling structural keywords “placeholders” — don’t bind widgets, configure UI behavior nodes; “--default” wrappers ’s one family — ppLayerOff(layer, hide=) toggles already-existing layer checkbox; ppVerbSwitch(verb, switch_on=, label=) creates stage checkbox (custom label) wouldn’t otherwise exist. Formula--source--truth principle (ADR 0020, refined ADR 0021): formula canonical record app shows boot every first-class UI control — placeholders’ initial values (ppText(initial = "hi")), layer-checkbox boot state (ppLayerOff(..., hide = TRUE)), stage-checkbox existence + boot state + label (ppVerbSwitch(verb, switch_on = FALSE, label = "…")). formula reads ggpaintr — naked-R semantics drop hidden layers, skip switched-verbs, ignore UI-label args. stage_id UI-routing key, execution-shape — LOCKED 2026-05-24 (ADR 0021): ptr_call carrying stage_id = "<layer>_<path>_stage_enabled" addressable UI checkbox; without stage_id (one whose stage_enabled[[sid]] reactive unbound non-FALSE) stage runs unconditionally. disable_walk drops stage iff isFALSE(stage_enabled[[sid]]) — NULL/missing reactives runtime -ops. Therefore stage_id differs two trees differ “stage UI checkbox?” — pure UI-affordance metadata. ptr_tree_structural_equal excludes stage_id alongside default_active / default_stage_enabled / has_user_control / stage_label / op / expr. supersedes ADR 0020’s --Scope note called stage_id “runtime-semantic”; framing based disable_walk can given stage_id, unwired case. Avoid: treating stage_id presence execution-shape difference; assuming stage_id stamping changes runtime behavior (doesn’t — bound FALSE reactive ); “stage_id runtime-semantic” (ADR-0020 framing overrides). Stage-checkbox gating (R/paintr-disable.R::is_data_chain_call): ptr_call qualifies stage_id iff subtree contains placeholder carries has_user_control = TRUE (stamped ppVerbSwitch). Single gate, two triggers — post-translate stamping pass stamp_default_stage_enabled_ids Plan 01 introduced workaround removed; gate work one place.

title: ggpaintr — Domain Context
text: ptr_<x> = L2 self-contained (owns .ptr-app + asset bundle; drop host app; shell remember). ptr_ui_<x> = L3 bare (emits widgets; L3 user supplies Shiny / shell). rule governs entire api-audit rename. earlier ptr_embed_* idea dead (superseded). L2 self-contained pair ptr_ui/ptr_server (renamed ptr_module_ui/ptr_module_server ADR 0006, voids ADR 0004/0005’s sealed-bundle name exception). now regular ptr_<x> self-contained names; ptr_server single public server entry (sibling “module” server exists, “module” qualifier dropped misleading). L3 public surface (bare): ptr_ui_controls, ptr_ui_plot, ptr_ui_error, ptr_ui_code, ptr_ui_header, ptr_ui_shared_panel, plus combinators (). what: 5. Inspecting what the LLM will see
title: Using ggpaintr from an LLM with ellmer
text: can read topic directly, without booting chat session, useful writing integration tests checking content release:
code: # Head of the primer cat(substr(ptr_llm_primer(), 1, 400), "\n...") #> # ggpaintr — R package for generating ggplot Shiny apps from formula strings #>  #> **Use ggpaintr — do NOT write raw Shiny — when the user asks in R for:** an interactive ggplot explorer, a dashboard with widgets tied to a ggplot, letting a user pick columns/labels/sizes for a plot, uploading a CSV / TSV / RDS / Excel / JSON file and plotting it. Raw `shiny::fluidPage()` for plot exploration is the wro  #> ... # A full topic payload cat(ptr_llm_topic("level1_ptr_app")) #> # Level 1 — turn-key app with `ptr_app()` #>  #> Use when: the user wants an interactive ggplot explorer and does not need to own the Shiny UI. No Shiny code is written. #>  #> ## Signature #>  #> ```r #> ptr_app( #>   formula, #>   envir          = parent.frame(), #>   ui_text        = NULL, #>   expr_check     = TRUE, #>   safe_to_remove = character(), #>   css            = NULL, #>   spec           = NULL #> ) #> ``` #>  #> There is no `placeholders =` argument: custom placeholder keywords are registered against a **process-global** registry via `ptr_define_placeholder_value()` / `_consumer()` / `_source()` before the app launches (see `custom_placeholder`). #>  #> ## Minimal example #>  #> ```r #> library(ggpaintr) #>  #> ptr_app( #>   "ggplot(iris, aes(ppVar, ppVar, color = ppVar)) + geom_point() + labs(title = ppText)" #> ) #> ``` #>  #> Every `ppVar`, `ppText` becomes a sidebar widget. Clicking **Update plot** re-renders the plot and refreshes the generated code on the side. #>  #> ## Single-instance shared widgets — no coordinator #>  #> One ggpaintr instance never sees `ptr_shared()` / `ptr_shared_panel()`. Every shared key is formula-local by definition and auto-renders in the inline shared section. Write the annotation and you are done: #>  #> ```r #> ptr_app( #>   "ggplot(iris, aes(x = ppVar(shared = 'col'), y = ppVar(shared = 'col'), #>                     color = Species)) + geom_point()" #> ) #> ``` #>  #> The standalone shared panel (and the coordinator that feeds it) only exists for **multiple** instances — see `level2_shared`. #>  #> ## Data sources — three paths #>  #> 1. **Named frame in the calling environment.** `data = iris` inside the formula string; `iris` is resolved via `envir`. #> 2. **`ppUpload` keyword.** Replace `data = iris` with `data = ppUpload` (or use `ppUpload` anywhere a data frame is needed); the user picks a `.csv`, `.tsv`, `.rds`, `.xlsx`, `.xls`, or `.json` file at runtime. #> 3. **Non-syntactic column names.** Wrap the frame with `ptr_normalize_column_names()` before passing it in; uploads get the same normalization automatically. #>  #> ## When to move up a level #>  #> - Need a specific Shiny layout but ggpaintr's default block is fine → Level 2 (`ptr_ui()` / `ptr_server()`; see `level2_module`). #> - Need multiple linked instances sharing one widget → Level 2 shared trio (see `level2_shared`). #> - Need to hand-place every pane, or render the plot yourself (Plotly, ggiraph), or post-process the ggplot object → Level 3 (`ptr_server()` returns the state; see `level3_layout` and `level3_custom_render`). #> - Need a widget type not in the five built-ins → custom placeholder (see `custom_placeholder`).

code: ptr_define_placeholder_consumer(   keyword = "colvars",    build_ui = function(node, cols = character(), data = NULL,                       label = NULL, selected = character(0), ...) {     shiny::selectInput(       node$id, label = label %||% "Columns",       choices  = cols,       selected = intersect(selected, cols),  # keep only still-valid picks       multiple = TRUE     )   },    resolve_expr = function(value, node, ...) {     if (length(value) == 0L) return(NULL)     rlang::call2("c", !!!as.list(value))   # c(col1, col2, ...)   },    parse_positional_arg = ptr_arg_symbol_or_string(),   ui_text_defaults = list(label = "Columns for {param}")   # validate_session_input / parse_named_args / embellish_eval: same shape as 2.1, omitted here. ) ptr_app(   mtcars |>     dplyr::select(colvars) |>     ggplot(aes(x = ppVar, y = ppVar)) + geom_point() )

title: Tutorial
text: source produces data rest formula reads, sits head pipeline. shared arguments (parse_positional_arg, parse_named_args, embellish_eval, ui_text_defaults) work exactly 2.1. Three things differ. resolve_data replaces resolve_expr required producer. function(value, node, ...) must return data frame. resolve_expr becomes optional defaults function(value, node, ...) rlang::sym(value) (symbol names produced frame generated code) — override need different generated code. embellish_eval defaults abort guard, identity — source sensible plain-R meaning give one. Override embellish_eval want formula runnable ordinary R. shortcut = TRUE adds framework-owned companion text box. set, ggpaintr renders sibling textInput (node$shortcut_id) user can type name object load app environment — “type dataset name” box saw ppUpload. framework owns box, build_ui must render node$id (, env-name-source, nothing ) — rendering shortcut bind id twice. ppDataset lets user type name data frame scope; slider/selector style widget unnecessary framework text box entry point. Note resolve_data runs later, inside framework — capture registration environment now (.env) load , rather reaching parent.frame() resolve time: source owns real widget (e.g. selectInput dataset names) shape shortcut = FALSE build_ui renders picker node$id.
code: .env <- environment()   # the scope whose data frames should be loadable  ptr_define_placeholder_source(   keyword  = "ppDataset",   shortcut = TRUE,    build_ui = function(node, label = NULL, ...) {     # env-name-only source: the framework's shortcut text box is the sole     # entry point, so build_ui contributes no widget of its own.     NULL   },    resolve_data = function(value, node, ...) {     nm <- if (is.character(value) && length(value) == 1L && nzchar(value)) value else NULL     if (is.null(nm)) return(NULL)     tryCatch(get(nm, envir = .env, inherits = TRUE),              error = function(e) NULL)   },    resolve_expr     = function(value, node, ...) rlang::sym(value),   ui_text_defaults = list(label = "Dataset for {param}") ) ptr_app(   ppDataset() |> ggplot(aes(x = ppVar("mpg"))) + geom_histogram() )

Put ptr_ui(formula, id) plot’s controls output go, ptr_server(formula, id) server matching id. ptr_server() namespaces — call bare, never wrapped moduleServer(): ptr_ui() ptr_server() take formula way ptr_app() : pass ggplot() call directly, — write hand formula — store rlang::expr() splice !!. what: Sharing one control across several plots
title: Tutorial
text: Often several plots driven control — one x-axis picker, one size slider — rather copy per plot. declare reserved shared = "<key>" argument placeholder. Placeholders carry key backed single widget. single widget placed depends many formulas reference key — partition: key used two formulas owned standalone shared panel sits plots drives . key used exactly one formula renders inline one plot’s controls. several plots build small coordinator object ptr_shared() hand three pieces: ptr_shared_panel() (standalone panel UI), ptr_ui(..., shared = obj), ptr_shared_server() (whose result thread ptr_server(..., shared_state = )). example two scatter plots iris. size slider shared formulas, lands standalone panel moves plots . plot’s x-axis picker shared within formula , renders inline plot: obj$panel_keys reports keys ended panel-owned — , "sz". Move size slider panel click panel’s draw button: plots re-render lockstep. x-axis picker, formula-local, changes plot. partition rule means single ggpaintr instance, reusing one shared key several times one formula needs coordinator — ggpaintr renders one inline widget wires every occurrence automatically. ptr_shared() panel multi-formula case. tutorial stops. tailoring labels copy, theming, trust model behind ppExpr ppUpload, see companion vignettes.
code: # A custom value placeholder for the shared size control: a 1-6 slider. ptr_define_placeholder_value(   keyword        = "ppSize",   parse_positional_arg = ptr_arg_numeric(),   build_ui = function(node, label = NULL, selected = NULL, ...) {     val <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(selected %||% node$default %||% 3))     if (length(val) != 1L || is.na(val)) val <- 3     shiny::sliderInput(node$id, label %||% "Size", min = 1, max = 6, value = val)   },   resolve_expr = function(value, node, ...) {     out <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(value))     if (length(out) != 1L || is.na(out)) NULL else out   } )  plots <- list(   rlang::expr(ggplot(iris, aes(x = ppVar(shared = "ax1"), y = Sepal.Width,                                color = Species)) + geom_point(size = ppSize(shared = "sz"))),   rlang::expr(ggplot(iris, aes(x = ppVar(shared = "ax2"), y = Petal.Width,                                color = Species)) + geom_point(size = ppSize(shared = "sz"))) )  obj <- ptr_shared(formulas = plots)        # a list of formulas, passed as-is obj$panel_keys           # "sz"  -- used by both formulas, so panel-owned  ui <- shiny::fluidPage(   ptr_shared_panel(obj),                 # holds the shared size slider   shiny::fluidRow(     shiny::column(6, ptr_ui(!!plots[[1]], "plot_1", shared = obj)),  # ax1 inline     shiny::column(6, ptr_ui(!!plots[[2]], "plot_2", shared = obj))   # ax2 inline   ) ) server <- function(input, output, session) {   sh <- ptr_shared_server(obj)   ptr_server(!!plots[[1]], "plot_1", shared_state = sh)   ptr_server(!!plots[[2]], "plot_2", shared_state = sh) } shiny::shinyApp(ui, server)

title: ggpaintr
text: single call returns running Shiny app. placeholder formula becomes one widget: three ppVar tokens → column pickers populated iris, ppNum → numeric input (point size), ppText → text input (plot title), ppExpr → code box facet spec (e.g. vars(Species)). library(ggpaintr) also attaches ggplot2, bare ggplot() / aes() / geom_*() calls work directly formula expression. reuse formula across calls, store rlang::expr() splice !! — f <- rlang::expr(ggplot(...)); ptr_app(!!f). string form (ptr_app("ggplot(...)")) remains supported fallback, handy formula built fetched text. swap custom page shell theme, write thin wrapper top public primitives. ptr_app() call needs Shiny session run, evaluated . pp* token plain identity function, formula without ptr_app() ordinary, runnable ggplot2 — drop placeholders plot, sketch static chart first, wrap ptr_app() want widgets:  ppVar(Sepal.Length) evaluates Sepal.Length ppNum(3) 3, chart renders exactly plain ggplot2 — tokens become widgets expression handed ptr_app(). Handing exact expression ptr_app() launches interactive app, value inside placeholder becomes widget’s initial selection — app boots x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width, color = Species, size = 3 pre-selected renders figure straight away, ready re-pointed columns: expression need inline. Capture rlang::expr() reuse — ptr_app() resolves formula stored variable name (!! needed), can build, inspect, share formula launching app:
code: library(ggpaintr)  # Primary form: pass the ggplot() call directly as an unquoted expression. ptr_app(   ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = ppVar, y = ppVar)) +     geom_point(aes(color = ppVar), size = ppNum) +     labs(title = ppText) +     facet_wrap(ppExpr) ) ggplot(iris, aes(x = ppVar(Sepal.Length), y = ppVar(Sepal.Width), color = ppVar(Species))) +   geom_point(size = ppNum(3)) +   labs(x = "Sepal.Length", y = "Sepal.Width", color = "Species") ptr_app(   ggplot(iris, aes(x = ppVar(Sepal.Length),                    y = ppVar(Sepal.Width),                     color = ppVar(Species))) +     geom_point(size = ppNum(3)) ) formula_iris <- rlang::expr(   ggplot(iris, aes(x = ppVar(Sepal.Length),                     y = ppVar(Sepal.Width),                     color = ppVar(Species))) +     geom_point(size = ppNum(3)) )  ptr_app(formula_iris)

title: Off-by-default layer wrapper — ppLayerOff
text: layer_expr ggplot2 layer expression (e.g. geom_point(), facet_wrap(~ cyl)). Evaluated hide = FALSE. hide length-1 logical literal (TRUE FALSE). ptr_app() formulas MUST literal — translator aborts non-literal formula remains single source truth app's boot state. Defaults TRUE.

title: Off-by-default layer wrapper — ppLayerOff
text: Outside ptr_app(): NULL hide = TRUE, otherwise evaluated layer_expr.

title: Off-by-default layer wrapper — ppLayerOff
text: Outside ptr_app() behaves per R semantics naked-ggplot scripts still render: ppLayerOff(geom_point(), TRUE) returns NULL (ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = mpg, y = wt)) + ppLayerOff(geom_point(), TRUE) renders without hidden layer); ppLayerOff(geom_point(), FALSE) returns layer. pipeline-stage sibling exposes user-toggleable checkbox (ADR-0021), see ppVerbSwitch().

title: Off-by-default layer wrapper — ppLayerOff
code: library(ggplot2) # Naked-R semantics: hide = TRUE drops the layer to NULL. p1 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = mpg, y = wt)) +   ppLayerOff(geom_point(), TRUE)        # equivalent to no geom_point p2 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = mpg, y = wt)) +   ppLayerOff(geom_point(), FALSE)       # the layer is added  # Inside ptr_app(), the wrapper becomes a node-level default and a # boot-state-off checkbox: # ptr_app(ggplot() + ppLayerOff(geom_point(aes(x = mpg, y = wt)), TRUE))

title: Switchable pipeline-stage wrapper — ppVerbSwitch
text: ADR-0021 structural keyword marks pipeline stage user- toggleable ptr_app(). boolean argument switch_on (positive sense: TRUE applies verb, FALSE skips ) optional label carries UI text resulting checkbox. Inside ptr_app() / ptr_server() parser sees wrapper unwraps translate time, stamping boot-state metadata + UI label onto resulting node. wrapper never appears typed tree.

title: Switchable pipeline-stage wrapper — ppVerbSwitch
code: ppVerbSwitch(.data, verb_expr, switch_on = TRUE, label = NULL)

title: Switchable pipeline-stage wrapper — ppVerbSwitch
text: .data data frame pipe-supplied dataset (implicit .data slot used pipeline stage). verb_expr data-pipeline verb call (e.g. mutate(mpg = mpg + 100), filter(cyl == 6)). Evaluated .data inserted first positional argument switch_on = TRUE. switch_on length-1 non-NA logical literal. ptr_app() formulas MUST literal — translator aborts non-literal formula remains single source truth app's boot state. Defaults TRUE (apply verb). label Optional length-1 character used checkbox label inside ptr_app(). Ignored naked-R path. Defaults NULL.

title: Switchable pipeline-stage wrapper — ppVerbSwitch
text: Outside ptr_app(): returns .data unchanged switch_on = FALSE, otherwise result verb_expr applied .data.

title: Switchable pipeline-stage wrapper — ppVerbSwitch
text: Outside ptr_app() behaves per R semantics naked-dplyr scripts still render: ppVerbSwitch(.data, mutate(x = 1), FALSE) returns .data unchanged; ppVerbSwitch(.data, mutate(x = 1), TRUE) routes .data verb call. label metadata-outside ptr_app() (naked-R path ignores ).

title: Switchable pipeline-stage wrapper — ppVerbSwitch
text: ppVerbSwitch(.data, verb_expr, switch_on = TRUE) inserts .data first positional argument verb_expr switch_on TRUE. matches tidyverse convention translator's pipeline-stage handling; non-tidyverse verbs take data later argument supported (use lambda stage named wrapper instead).

title: Switchable pipeline-stage wrapper — ppVerbSwitch
code: # Naked-R semantics: switch_on = FALSE leaves the data unchanged. identical(   ppVerbSwitch(mtcars, dplyr::mutate(mpg = mpg + 100), FALSE),   mtcars ) #> [1] TRUE  # switch_on = TRUE routes .data through the verb. result <- ppVerbSwitch(mtcars, dplyr::filter(mpg > 20), TRUE) nrow(result)  # 14 #> [1] 14  # Inside ptr_app(), the wrapper becomes a node-level default + a # labelled boot-state-on checkbox (plans 03-05 wire the UI): # ptr_app("mtcars |> ppVerbSwitch(filter(mpg > 20), TRUE, label = 'Filter')")

Outside ptr_app() behave plain R functions: first four return argument unchanged (identity), formula aes(x = ppVar(mpg)) evaluates identically aes(x = mpg) ggplot2's tidy-eval. ppUpload identical called argument (ppUpload(penguins) returns penguins), formula ppUpload(penguins) |> filter(...) |> ggplot(...) evaluates plain R penguins scope. -arg form ppUpload() aborts outside ptr_app() — meaningful placeholder slot.

title: Build a Shiny App from a ggpaintr Formula — ptr_app
text: formula Either single character scalar containing ggplot expression ggpaintr placeholders, unquoted ggplot expression supplied directly. Expression-mode captured rlang::enexpr() public boundary, deparsed string reaching shared translate pipeline; modes produce equivalent apps. bare symbol bound string calling frame (e.g. f <- "..."; ptr_app(f)) resolved treated string mode. Pre-quoted wrappers (rlang::expr(), rlang::quo(), base::quote(), base::bquote()) captured root unwrapped one level. !! splicing inside captured expression honoured via rlang::enexpr(). Native pipe (|>) caveat: expression mode, R's parser desugars |> capture, rendered code shows desugared nested-call form. Stay string mode (use %>%) need |> preserved. envir Environment used resolve local data objects. ui_text Optional named list copy overrides; see ptr_ui_text() full schema current defaults. expr_check Controls ppExpr placeholder validation. Three modes: TRUE (default) applies built-denylist + AST walker; FALSE disables validation (local prototyping trusted input ); list deny_list /allow_list entries (character vectors) customises policy without disabling . See vignette("ggpaintr-safety") walker model. safe_to_remove Character vector additional function names whose zero-argument calls dropped placeholder substitution leaves empty. Defaults character(). See Empty-call cleanup . user-typed ppExpr always wins — whatever user enters ppExpr box honoured verbatim, even top-level name safe_to_remove. css Optional character vector paths additional CSS files. served static resource linked ggpaintr's bundled stylesheet, rules override default .ptr-* styling. Relative url(...) references inside file resolve file's directory. Defaults NULL (extra stylesheet). spec optional named list fully-qualified Shiny input id -> value, used override widget defaults session boot. See ADR 0012.

title: Build a Shiny App from a ggpaintr Formula — ptr_app

title: Build a Shiny App from a ggpaintr Formula — ptr_app
text: ggpaintr formula single ggplot() call written string. Drop one five placeholder keywords anywhere value normally go, runtime substitutes user's input back expression render time. ppVar Column picker, data-aware. Renders selectInput populated upstream data's column-name vector. Example: aes(x = ppVar). ppText Free-text input. Renders textInput. Example: labs(title = text). ppNum Numeric input. Renders numericInput. Example: geom_point(size = ppNum). ppExpr Code editor, validated expr_check. keyword accepts arbitrary R code; see vignette("ggpaintr-safety") model. Example: facet_wrap(ppExpr). ppUpload File picker, returns data frame. Renders fileInput plus optional dataset-name textbox. Accepted formats: .csv, .tsv, .rds, .xlsx, .xls, .json. Uploaded data normalized via ptr_normalize_column_names() automatically. Example: ggplot(ppUpload, ...). keyword occurrence may carry shared = "<id>" lift widget per-layer panel top-level shared section. Used ptr_app_grid() drive multiple plots one control. See vignette("ggpaintr-tutorial") worked examples keyword.

title: Build a Shiny App from a ggpaintr Formula — ptr_app
text: placeholder resolves "missing" (empty ppVar pick, blank ppText, cleared ppNum, unchecked layer checkbox), argument dropped generated code. surrounding call left empty bare name curated cleanup list, whole call disappears . rule applies placeholder-driven empties user-authored literal empty calls like + labs(). Curated ggplot2 names dropped empty: Empty calls similar -op helpers dplyr, tidyr, tibble, pillar, purrr, stringr, forcats, lubridate, hms covered rule. geom_*() stat_*() layers never dropped, regardless whether end empty — inherit aesthetics ggplot() remain meaningful arguments. element_blank() intentionally cleanup list: empty form meaningful "suppress" directive, -op. Third-party helpers (e.g. pcp_theme() ggpcp) cleanup list — absent "removal safety unknown" signal. Use safe_to_remove = c("pcp_theme") opt specific name .

what: Bslib-Themed App: A Demonstration Wrapper — ptr_app_bslib

title: Argument validators for placeholder definitions (ptr_arg_*) — ptr_arg_validators
text: validators operate AST : call eval(), parse(), deparse--reparse cycle input. numeric helper ptr_arg_numeric() (scalar default, vector via vector = TRUE) walks AST constant-fold allowlist registry (see ptr_register_constant_fold()) evaluate sealed environment whose bindings registered names. Symbol policy per-helper: ptr_arg_symbol_or_string() accepts bareword symbol (returned character name, preserving non-syntactic / backticked names) single string literal (including empty string). ptr_arg_symbol() accepts bareword symbol (returned character name); rejects string literals, numbers, compound calls. ptr_arg_string() accepts single string literal (including empty string); rejects symbols numbers. ptr_arg_numeric() accepts AST whose every node syntactic literal registered constant-fold name; default scalar mode result must length-one non-NA numeric. vector form use ptr_arg_numeric(vector = TRUE, length = NULL). ptr_arg_expression() verbatim store: returns input unchanged can later evaluated data context. convenience emits one-shot warning user wraps expression quote(), bquote(), rlang::ppExpr(), rlang::quo() (wrapper stored verbatim). ptr_arg_symbol_or_string(), ptr_arg_symbol(), ptr_arg_string(), ptr_arg_numeric() takes vector flag. vector = FALSE (default) validator parses single scalar element returns length-one value. vector = TRUE parses c(...) literal element--element (element subject helper's scalar element rule) returns whole vector; lone element treated length-one vector. ptr_arg_numeric(vector = TRUE) optional length check (honored vector mode) asserts parsed vector's length.

Widget-seeding contract — selected. precedence, omit---default, render-empty--empty, never-read- node$default rules value placeholders — see "Widget-seeding contract" block ptr_define_placeholder_value() authoritative description. Consumer-specific rule: filter intersect(). Always pass selected = intersect(selected %||% character(0), cols) (equivalent column filter) underlying widget, bare selected. Three cases collapse one line: User-cleared widget (selected empty) → intersect() returns character(0), widget renders empty. Honors render-empty--empty rule. Stale pick upstream data swap (selected names column longer cols) → intersect() drops cleanly. Without , selectInput silently falls back first choice (silent data mutation) shinyWidgets::pickerInput enters similarly broken state. Valid pick → intersect() -op, value flows . resolve_expr function(value, node, ...). column picker typical body rlang::sym(value) bare column name spliced identifier rather string literal. See ptr_define_placeholder_value() allowed return types NULL-prunes--argument convention. validate_session_input Optional function(value, ctx) called resolve_expr. Return TRUE / NULL accept; return single character string reject (surfaced inline error message, layer pruned). Useful stale selection longer matches upstream column data swap, certain column types admissible. ctx plain list named fields: node (placeholder AST node, carries $id, $keyword, $args), keyword (convenience alias node$keyword), upstream_cols (character vector upstream column names — value build_ui received cols), data (upstream data frame — object build_ui received data). ctx$upstream_cols ctx$data may NULL upstream resolution pending; validator invoked upstream yet resolved (substitute walker skips hook case). ggpaintr invokes function validate_input(value, ctx) — positional named arguments passed, ctx carries exactly four fields . signature require .... parse_positional_arg, parse_named_args See ptr_define_placeholder_value(). Consumer placeholders use arg-schema slots; ppVar built-passes column-name validator used ppVar(mpg). embellish_eval Optional function(x, ...) body used placeholder called plain-R function. title: Define a data-consumer placeholder (e.g. column picker) — ptr_define_placeholder_consumer
code: # A consumer that picks a numeric-only column. # Note: `selected = NULL` formal (per the seeding contract) plus # intersect() filter (per the consumer-specific rule). Empty input # renders empty; a stale pick after a data swap drops cleanly. ptr_define_placeholder_consumer(   keyword = "numvar",   build_ui = function(node, cols, data, label = NULL,                       selected = NULL, ...) {     numeric_cols <- if (is.null(data)) character(0) else       names(data)[vapply(data, is.numeric, logical(1))]     retained <- intersect(selected %||% character(0), numeric_cols)     shiny::selectInput(node$id, label = label %||% "Numeric column",                        choices = numeric_cols, selected = retained)   },   resolve_expr = function(value, node, ...) {     if (length(value) != 1L || !nzchar(value)) return(NULL)     rlang::sym(value)   },   validate_session_input = function(value, ctx) {     if (length(value) == 1L && value %in% ctx$upstream_cols) TRUE     else "Pick a column that exists in the upstream data."   } ) #> function (x, ...)  #> x #> <bytecode: 0x55f201511e48> #> <environment: 0x55f20151e910> ptr_clear_placeholder("numvar") #> ✔ Cleared placeholder: "numvar".

title: Define a value placeholder — ptr_define_placeholder_value
code: # A percentage placeholder: user types a number 0-100; we splice # the fraction 0-1 into the rendered call. The hook reads `selected` # to honor a formula default like `pct(75)` at boot and to keep a # user-cleared widget empty across Update Plot fires (do NOT # substitute a hardcoded fallback when selected is empty). ptr_define_placeholder_value(   keyword = "pct",   build_ui = function(node, label = NULL, selected = NULL, ...) {     n <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(selected))     initial <- if (length(n) == 1L && is.finite(n)) n else NA_real_     shiny::numericInput(node$id, label = label %||% "Percent",                         value = initial, min = 0, max = 100, step = 1)   },   resolve_expr = function(value, node, ...) {     if (length(value) != 1L || !is.finite(value)) return(NULL)     value / 100   },   parse_positional_arg = ptr_arg_numeric(),  # accept ppPct(50)-style defaults   ui_text_defaults = list(label = "Percent for {param}") ) #> function (x, ...)  #> x #> <bytecode: 0x55f201511e48> #> <environment: 0x55f200257ee0> ptr_clear_placeholder("pct") #> ✔ Cleared placeholder: "pct".

code: ptr_init_state(   formula,   envir = parent.frame(),   ui_text = NULL,   expr_check = TRUE,   safe_to_remove = character(),   shared = list(),   draw_trigger = NULL,   producer_debounce_ms = NULL,   ns = shiny::NS(NULL),   server_ns = ns,   auto_bind_shared = FALSE,   shared_resolutions = list(),   shared_stage_enabled = list(),   panel_sources = list(),   plots = NULL )

title: Construct the ggpaintr runtime state container — ptr_init_state
text: formula single formula string ggpaintr placeholders. envir Environment used resolve local data objects. ui_text Optional named list copy overrides; see ptr_ui_text() full schema current defaults. expr_check Controls ppExpr placeholder validation: TRUE (default) applies built-denylist + AST walker; FALSE disables validation; list deny_list/allow_list entries customises policy. See vignette("ggpaintr-safety"). safe_to_remove Character vector additional function names whose zero-argument calls dropped substitution. shared Named list reactives (one per shared key) supplied outer wrapper ptr_app_grid(). Defaults list(). draw_trigger Optional reactive whose invalidation forces redraw (e.g. grid app's "Draw " button). producer_debounce_ms Optional. Controls debounce window applied producer-style placeholder inputs (ppText, ppNum, ppExpr) invalidate downstream consumer caches. NULL (default) enables auto mode: window starts 0 ms runtime flips 300 ms three consecutive upstream resolutions exceed 150 ms (back 0 five consecutive resolutions 80 ms). Pass 0 force forever, positive integer pin manual window. ns namespace function used rendered ids (UI side). server_ns namespace function used server-side input lookups. Defaults ns. auto_bind_shared TRUE, host (single-plot ptr_app() ptr_app_grid() auto-render path) binds shared widgets host scope. Relaxes "missing--bindings" check ptr_validate_shared_bindings() (host auto-binds instead). shared_resolutions Named list (keyed raw shared key) host-computed resolutions shared data-consumer (ppVar) widgets, returned ptr_resolve_shared_consumers(). entry present, runtime validates key's selection host-resolved upstream (data host picker built ) instead per-layer node$upstream, value valid host picker never rejected one layer's narrower upstream. Defaults list() (host resolutions; per-layer behaviour). shared_stage_enabled Named list (keyed raw shared key) reactives, returning logical, toggle orphan pipeline stages owned shared key (carried ptr_shared_server() bundle). missing unset entry leaves stage enabled. Defaults list(). panel_sources Named list (keyed source-node id) reactives, returning host-loaded data panel-owned shared source (ADR 0023). Populated host's ptr_setup_panel_sources() threaded ptr_shared_server() bundle per-instance binders read host's primed data (state$panel_sources[[node$id]]) instead re-wiring source UI. Defaults list() (single-plot / per-instance context — panel-owned sources). plots Optional list formula strings grid contexts. supplied (typically ptr_app_grid()), validator shared bindings cross-checks shared-key references every plot's placeholder set, shared = "..." annotation exists plot 2 plot 1 still validates. Defaults NULL (single- plot context — formula checked).

title: Fetch a ggpaintr LLM topic by name — ptr_llm_topic
code: cat(ptr_llm_topic("level1_ptr_app")) #> # Level 1 — turn-key app with `ptr_app()` #>  #> Use when: the user wants an interactive ggplot explorer and does not need to own the Shiny UI. No Shiny code is written. #>  #> ## Signature #>  #> ```r #> ptr_app( #>   formula, #>   envir          = parent.frame(), #>   ui_text        = NULL, #>   expr_check     = TRUE, #>   safe_to_remove = character(), #>   css            = NULL, #>   spec           = NULL #> ) #> ``` #>  #> There is no `placeholders =` argument: custom placeholder keywords are registered against a **process-global** registry via `ptr_define_placeholder_value()` / `_consumer()` / `_source()` before the app launches (see `custom_placeholder`). #>  #> ## Minimal example #>  #> ```r #> library(ggpaintr) #>  #> ptr_app( #>   "ggplot(iris, aes(ppVar, ppVar, color = ppVar)) + geom_point() + labs(title = ppText)" #> ) #> ``` #>  #> Every `ppVar`, `ppText` becomes a sidebar widget. Clicking **Update plot** re-renders the plot and refreshes the generated code on the side. #>  #> ## Single-instance shared widgets — no coordinator #>  #> One ggpaintr instance never sees `ptr_shared()` / `ptr_shared_panel()`. Every shared key is formula-local by definition and auto-renders in the inline shared section. Write the annotation and you are done: #>  #> ```r #> ptr_app( #>   "ggplot(iris, aes(x = ppVar(shared = 'col'), y = ppVar(shared = 'col'), #>                     color = Species)) + geom_point()" #> ) #> ``` #>  #> The standalone shared panel (and the coordinator that feeds it) only exists for **multiple** instances — see `level2_shared`. #>  #> ## Data sources — three paths #>  #> 1. **Named frame in the calling environment.** `data = iris` inside the formula string; `iris` is resolved via `envir`. #> 2. **`ppUpload` keyword.** Replace `data = iris` with `data = ppUpload` (or use `ppUpload` anywhere a data frame is needed); the user picks a `.csv`, `.tsv`, `.rds`, `.xlsx`, `.xls`, or `.json` file at runtime. #> 3. **Non-syntactic column names.** Wrap the frame with `ptr_normalize_column_names()` before passing it in; uploads get the same normalization automatically. #>  #> ## When to move up a level #>  #> - Need a specific Shiny layout but ggpaintr's default block is fine → Level 2 (`ptr_ui()` / `ptr_server()`; see `level2_module`). #> - Need multiple linked instances sharing one widget → Level 2 shared trio (see `level2_shared`). #> - Need to hand-place every pane, or render the plot yourself (Plotly, ggiraph), or post-process the ggplot object → Level 3 (`ptr_server()` returns the state; see `level3_layout` and `level3_custom_render`). #> - Need a widget type not in the five built-ins → custom placeholder (see `custom_placeholder`).

title: ggpaintr 0.11.0
text: four public placeholder-constructor arguments renamed across ptr_define_placeholder_value() / ptr_define_placeholder_consumer() / ptr_define_placeholder_source(): positional_arg → parse_positional_arg, named_args → parse_named_args, validate_input → validate_session_input, runtime → embellish_eval. rename makes slot family legible — two slots parse formula’s default args widget seed (parse_positional_arg, parse_named_args), one validates live session value (validate_session_input), one defines plain-R evaluation embellished expression (embellish_eval). hard break deprecation shim: passing old name now raises R’s “unused argument” error. internal registry-record keys unchanged (entry$default_arg / named_args / validate_input / runtime); public argument names changed. embellish_eval defaults new embellish_identity() value/consumer placeholders (source role keeps abort-guard default). Two built-helpers ship plain-R evaluation slot: embellish_identity() (identity function(x, ...) x) embellish_symbol_to_string() (NSE-captures argument returns column names character vector, column-selecting consumer works inside tidyselect verb placeholder-embellished formula run ordinary R app). ptr_arg_numeric_vector() removed. Use ptr_arg_numeric(vector = TRUE). argument-validator factories (ptr_arg_numeric / ptr_arg_string / ptr_arg_symbol_or_string) gained vector = FALSE flag (length = honored vector mode ptr_arg_numeric), new ptr_arg_symbol() factory parses bareword column name (vector-capable) multi-column positional defaults. ptr_app_bslib() ptr_app_grid() longer exported (changed @keywords internal). remain available internally help pages still generated existing cross-reference links resolve, longer presented public API; public turn-key entry point ptr_app(). Write thin wrapper public primitives custom page shell theme.

title: ggpaintr 0.11.0
text: RStudio command-palette addin. ptr_wrap_placeholder_addin() wraps highlighted selection placeholder RStudio command palette (classifies selection, offers placeholder picker, rewrites place; dark/light-aware via rstudioapi::getThemeInfo()). Documentation now leads expression form. Roxygen @examples formula-capturing entry points (ptr_app, ptr_ui_controls, ptr_ui_page, ptr_server) lead unquoted ggplot expression (canonical input), keeping string form documented fallback; Shiny examples gated (interactive()).

title: ggpaintr 0.11.0
text: ~ formula treated first-class atomic canonical-pipeline lift, resugar pass longer shreds formula inside ppExpr (e.g. lm(ppExpr(y ~ x), data = ppUpload(d))); placeholders inside formula still detected wired translate_call() recurses every argument independently. two-sided formula renders infix ~ (gear ~ cyl) instead falling prefix ~(gear, cyl), explicit parenthesis node (x) now renders balanced (x) instead unparseable ((x).

title: ggpaintr 0.10.0
text: ptr_shared() now accepts quoted ggplot expressions formulas elements, alongside strings. element formulas may formula string () quoted expression built rlang::expr() / quote(); quoted expressions deparsed source two forms interchangeable, including single mixed list. brings multi-formula coordinator line string--expression input already accepted ptr_app() / ptr_server(). built ggplot object (whose source text unrecoverable) rejected message pointing expr(). Additive fully backward-compatible — existing -string call sites unchanged. (single-formula entries, native pipe |> inside quoted expression desugared R capture survive generated code panel.)

title: ggpaintr 0.10.0
text: Auto-name (§2). Every source node now carries node$auto_name, stamped translate time non-shared sources (node$default %||% node$id) runtime shared sources (paste0(obj$id, "_", key)). shortcut textbox empty, substitute_pass splices .name(node$auto_name) rendered call always references real binding. UI mutex (§3). ppUpload, picking file synchronously clears shortcut textbox (auto-name takes binding name); typing textbox synchronously clears pending file. Typing always means “look object caller env”, never “rename uploaded payload”. Code-panel prologue (§4). Every active upload emits one-line prologue top code panel — <auto-name> <- read.csv("<filename>") (read.delim, readxl::read_excel, jsonlite::fromJSON, … per extension) — rendered snippet reads top--bottom self-contained script. Vacate--empty + spec round-trip (§5, §7 A1). Clearing shortcut textbox file present synchronously vacates state$bound_names[[key]], state$eval_env[[key]], state$active_uploads[[key]]. ptr_spec() dumps node$auto_name ("") bound name whenever textbox empty, reloading spec re-establishes binding running app saw.

title: ggpaintr 0.10.0
text: Two ptr_shared(..., id = ...) coordinators one page sharing panel-owned source key longer collide bare DOM ids. panel-shared ppUpload(shared = '<key>') rendered coordinator non-NULL id, inner fileInput + shortcut textInput still bound bare shared_<key> / shared_<key>_shortcut ids — second coordinator (id = "right") page emitted duplicate ids silently failed bind one two uploads. rendered widget now binds coordinator-namespaced ids (left-shared_<key> / right-shared_<key>), matching sibling render paths invoke_build_ui() ptr_setup_source_uis(). Single-coordinator apps (id = supplied) byte--byte unchanged.

what: Known limitation — ADR 0025 §7 A2 deferred (400 ms shortcut debounce)
title: ggpaintr 0.10.0
text: ptr_define_placeholder_source(shortcut = TRUE) reads shortcut textbox every keystroke. Two implementation attempts ADR’s proposed 400 ms debounce (shiny::debounce() shortcut input read) broke unrelated test contracts — bind-path delay broke test-rewrite-pipeline-data-source.R (6 FAIL) mutex-file-reset race test-shared-source-panel-multi-instance.R (2 FAIL). debounce therefore shipped release. User-visible impact: typing partial, unresolvable name shortcut textbox may briefly surface transient Object 'm' found-style error inline error pane typing burst; bind settles user finishes typing. debounce--race-preservation variant tracked follow-; A1 (vacate--empty) unaffected ships release. - validate_input hook signature unified across value consumer placeholders. ptr_define_placeholder_value() ptr_define_placeholder_consumer() now accept validate_input = function(value, ctx). consumer’s previous function(value, upstream_cols) signature gone — registrations still use fail loudly via validate_hook() (“must accept argument(s): ctx”). ctx plain list named fields: node (placeholder AST node), keyword (alias node$keyword), upstream_cols (character vector NULL), data (upstream data frame NULL). value placeholders, ctx$upstream_cols ctx$data always NULL (value placeholders upstream column scope definition); consumer placeholders, fields populated upstream resolves, letting data-aware validators inspect column types / ranges / levels just column names. Migration: rename second formal upstream_cols ctx read ctx$upstream_cols body. Value placeholders previously validator gain option declare one.

title: ggpaintr 0.10.0
text: Placeholder keywords renamed var / num / text / expr / upload ppVar / ppNum / ppText / ppExpr / ppUpload. Every formula passed ptr_app() / ptr_server() / ptr_translate() must use new vocabulary. pp-prefix avoids shadowing base R / ggplot2 names (stats::var(), graphics::text(), base::expression, etc.) lets symbols double identity / guard plain-R functions exported package — ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = ppVar(mpg))) works inside ptr_app() (placeholder) standalone ggplot call (ppVar identity, aes(x = ppVar(mpg)) evaluates aes(x = mpg)). See ADR 0009 full rationale. Migration: rename every placeholder call inside formula strings unquoted-expression formulas. Old keyword names longer reserved. names var, num, text, expr, upload longer registered ggpaintr longer trigger shadow-check guard, users previously custom placeholders blocked built-ins names can now register without conflict. Shiny input ids changed. ggpaintr’s internal Shiny input ids part public API; keyword rename shifted (e.g. ggplot_1_1_var_NA now ggplot_1_1_ppVar_NA). Bookmarked URLs / setBookmarkExclude allow-lists / setInputs() test fixtures previous versions need updated.

title: ggpaintr 0.10.0
text: Default-value arguments code-mode toggle (ADR 0009). Built-placeholders now accept positional argument seeds widget’s initial value: ppVar(mpg) opens column picker pre-selected mpg, ppNum(5) seeds numeric input 5, ppText("hello") pre-fills text input, ppExpr(factor(cyl)) pre-fills expression box. Defaults survive rename round-trip: ptr_render(root, preserve_placeholders = TRUE) re-emits original ppX(default) call rather substituted value. ptr_app() code panel gained “Final code” / “Show placeholders” toggle switches two render modes. Custom placeholders can opt default/named-args schema. ptr_define_placeholder_value() / ptr_define_placeholder_consumer() / ptr_define_placeholder_source() now accept default_arg = <validator> named_args = list(<name> = <validator>, ...). Use bundled validator factories — ptr_default_symbol_or_string(), ptr_default_numeric(), ptr_default_numeric_vector(), ptr_default_string(), ptr_default_expression() — pass custom closure. See ?ptr_define_placeholder_value. L3 — every piece UI. Every piece ggpaintr’s public UI now exported builder: ptr_ui_header(), ptr_ui_controls(), ptr_ui_plot(), ptr_ui_error(), ptr_ui_code(), ptr_ui_code_toggle(), ptr_ui_assets(). Compose exactly pieces want, place anywhere layout, wire existing server API (ptr_server() / ptr_module_server()). bundled ptr_app() / ptr_module_ui() / ptr_outputs_ui() / ptr_controls_ui() paths reimplemented thin composites pieces — <body> DOM, behavior performance change. pieces deliberately bare (assets, .ptr-app wrapper); new ptr_ui_page() shell wraps Bootstrap page + single .ptr-app theme scope + (deduped) asset bundle one call, L3 user remember. Swap page builder page = (fluidPage default, also fixedPage/fillPage/bootstrapPage/basicPage); navbarPage bslib root use documented decomposition recipe. See vignette("ggpaintr-use-cases") § “L3 — every piece UI”. ptr_server() now accepts ptr_shared_server() bundle directly via shared_state =, mirroring ptr_module_server(). Wiring page-level ptr_shared_ui() panel single embedded custom-rendered plot longer requires spreading four bundle slots .... custom-renderer pattern (reading state$runtime() renderPlotly() / renderGirafe()) now documented L2 capability — ptr_server() ptr_module_server() return ptr_state, needs nothing beyond embedding.

title: ggpaintr 0.10.0
text: ptr_register_plot() / ptr_register_error() / ptr_register_code() longer exported. Post-rewrite ever ran inside ptr_server() (sole caller, via internal runtime setup) rendered nothing without , “compose outputs manually” use case advertised impossible. remain internal helpers; replacement needed (use L3 pieces + ptr_server(), read state$runtime() custom renderer). deprecation cycle — dev cycle’s surface, single pre-1.0 user. ggpaintr’s CSS/JS now ship deduped htmltools::htmlDependency() bundles. ggpaintr.css, code-window JS, ptr_set_class handler + stage CSS emitted two dependencies (ggpaintr, ggpaintr-layer) instead inline <style>/<script>/<link> tags. htmltools collapses single <head> injection matter many shells/pieces page nests, old window.__ptr_*_registered JS guards gone. Rendered <body> unchanged; <head> asset emission differs. htmltools moves Suggests Imports. hook signatures changed : build_ui(node, label = NULL, ...) value/source, build_ui(node, cols, label = NULL, selected = character(0), ...) consumer. id node$id (, sources registered shortcut = TRUE, sibling-input id node$shortcut_id — see ADR 0025); separate id argument. resolve_expr(value, node, ...) returning NULL new “drop argument” signal (replacing ptr_missing_expr()). Distribution: package authors ship setup function calls ptr_define_placeholder_*() (caller-driven), auto-register .onLoad (drop-). Either pattern works; pick whether want registration visible call site. Multi-instance limitation: two ptr_module_server() instances Shiny app different implementations keyword. second registration overwrites first (cli::cli_warn notice). need divergent widgets per instance, prefix custom keywords package-specific tag (mypkg_pct rather pct). scoped per-instance registry override table future release. Test isolation: tests register custom placeholders restore state ptr_registry_clear() + ptr_register_builtins() (currently internal) run fresh R session. Extended curated empty-call cleanup list 14 ggplot2 names: annotation_custom(), annotation_map(), annotation_raster() (required args make zero-arg calls error stock ggplot2 — dropping prevents crash); aes(), aes_(), aes_q(), aes_string(), vars() (empty mapping helpers render-identical “absent” via inherit.aes); element_text(), element_line(), element_rect(), element_point(), element_polygon(), element_geom() (empty form inherits active theme element). means e.g. geom_point(aes(colour = var)) var missing now reduces + geom_point() (clean) instead + geom_point(aes()). element_blank() intentionally excluded — empty form meaningful “suppress” directive, -op. Replaced old name-prefix heuristic pruning empty calls. new rule : zero-argument call dropped iff bare function name curated ggplot2 cleanup list (theme(), labs(), xlab/ylab/ggtitle, facet_wrap/facet_grid/facet_null, xlim/ylim/lims, expand_limits, guides, annotate) new safe_to_remove argument ptr_app(), ptr_app_bslib(), ptr_app_components(), ptr_server(), ptr_module_server(), ptr_server_state(), ptr_complete_expr(), ptr_exec(). Empty calls whose name set (e.g. third-party helpers like pcp_theme(), pcp_arrange(), user-authored aes_pcp()) preserved default — absent set “removal safety unknown” signal. Pass safe_to_remove = c("pcp_theme") opt specific name cleanup pass. expr placeholder, user supplies expression, always wins cleanup pass: whatever user typed expr input honoured verbatim, even top-level name safe_to_remove. intent (“want ”) overrides curated list. geom_*() stat_*() layers kept empty regardless safe_to_remove, since inherit aesthetics ggplot(). geom_point(colour = var) var missing still renders geom_point(). Behavior change vs. prior implementation: user-authored literal + labs(), + theme(), + guides() calls now dropped (semantic change — ’re -ops stock ggplot2). previous build kept via “diff guard” removed favour cleaner curated-list rule.

title: ggpaintr 0.9.1
text: ptr_resolve_layer_data() ptr_ns_id() now exported full docs. Custom data-aware placeholders call helpers bind_ui() callback instead replicating source-symbol walk namespace-function dance. ?ptr_define_placeholder now documents bind_ui contract, contents placeholder context, meta$id namespacing rule. second runnable example demonstrates data-aware “numeric-columns-” placeholder using public helpers.

title: ggpaintr 0.9.0
text: ptr_gg_extra() — advanced helper embedded apps renderPlot({...}) block. Captures ggplot components (themes, scales, coords, …) added top ptr_extract_plot() stores new ptr_state$extras reactiveVal, default code binder (ptr_register_code()) appends generated-code pane. Extras suppressed automatically underlying runtime reports failure, stale extras prior successful draw never surface error state. See vignette("ggpaintr-extensibility"), Recipe 3. ptr_llm_primer(), ptr_llm_topic(), ptr_llm_topics(), ptr_llm_register() — ellmer-facing helpers expose bundled inst/llm/ primer topic files plain strings registered ellmer tools, coding assistants can fetch just--time ggpaintr guidance. See vignette("ggpaintr-llm"). upload placeholder now accepts .tsv, .xlsx, .xls, .json addition .csv .rds. JSON uploads must array records; nested objects flattened, nested arrays error . Excel JSON readers require new suggested packages readxl jsonlite. “show code” pane reflects format-appropriate reader (read.delim, readxl::read_excel, jsonlite::fromJSON).

title: ggpaintr 0.9.0
text: Removed Shiny app export feature: ptr_generate_shiny(), ptr_register_export(), download button, ids$export_button gone. Use public Shiny integration surface (ptr_server_state(), ptr_register_*(), ptr_input_ui(), ptr_output_ui(), ptr_app_bslib()) compose distribute custom apps. placeholder distribution parameters source_file, source_package, source_function, on_missing removed ptr_define_placeholder(). existed support exporter. Hook functions defined inline. last commit removals tagged v0-pre-export-removal.

title: ggpaintr 0.1.0
text: repositioned package around maintained ggpaintr workflow shortened API prefix: exported functions now use ptr_* prefix conciseness (previously ggpaintr_*) improved semantic clarity function names across public API (e.g., bind_* → register_*, *_value → extract_*) renamed copy_rules parameter/system ui_text throughout migrated error signaling base::stop() rlang::abort() added @examples exported functions clarified maintained public API boundary README pkgdown-facing docs beginner path stays centered wrapper, integration, export, placeholder, intentionally exported runtime helpers removed generated documentation topics package-internal helper functions internal implementation details longer presented part public community-facing surface updated ptr_generate_shiny() maintained public call path now ptr_generate_shiny(ptr_obj, output_file, ...) Note: ptr_generate_shiny() removed 0.9.0 — see breaking changes . archived legacy package implementation archive/legacy-package/ replaced legacy package docs, vignette, pkgdown content ggpaintr-first documentation added roxygen2-based package documentation active implementation prepared package structure R CMD check CRAN-oriented cleanup
